dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Exceptional prompt quality. Identity 68w, instructions cover 10 detailed operational steps totaling 800+ words, tool guidance includes specific API endpoints and headers, 3 rich examples covering happy path, follow-up, and rejection, and extensive error handling with edge cases.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request, file_write, file_read all referenced with specific usage patterns. Tool guidance maps precisely to Supabase, Slack, and Telegram APIs. No phantom tools.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: 4 triggers with distinct purposes: webhook for real-time Slack, polling for Telegram, weekly digest, daily schema refresh. All cron expressions valid.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: All 3 services (Supabase, Slack, Telegram) have connector definitions with credential fields, setup instructions, and related tools.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Conversation context with sliding window of 5 interactions per user/channel. Schema cache with TTL. Query log with monthly rotation. Excellent stateful design.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Covers API failures (timeout, rate limit, auth), query failures (syntax, permissions, table not found), data integrity (corrupted cache, context), and edge cases (ambiguous questions, large results, non-English, multiple questions).

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: 4 flows cover question answering, schema discovery/caching, weekly digest, and error recovery. All prompt workflows mapped to flows.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Replaces expensive BI tools for ad-hoc queries. Clear value prop: anyone can ask data questions in plain English.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: Supabase, Slack, Telegram all have builtin connectors. Requires a read-only SQL execution function in Supabase which adds setup complexity.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Conversation memory for follow-ups, safety validation pipeline, and schema-aware SQL generation go well beyond simple automation.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: 4 adoption questions are all genuinely needed for the agent to function (database URL, table access, channels).

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Defaults are good for Slack channel (#data-questions) and table access (*). However, supabase_url has no default — requires user input. Telegram defaults to disabled.

issues: No adoption_questions existed — connectors need user-specific configuration for Supabase URL, table allowlist, and channel names

template: Added 4 adoption_questions: supabase_url, allowed_tables, slack_channel, telegram_enabled
